title: # How to Use the Same Shortcuts Across Dual Systems   For users with dual systems (e.g., Windows + macOS or Linux + Windows), maintaining consistent shortcuts can significantly improve workflow efficiency. Below are some methods to achieve this.   ## 1. **Use Cross-Platform Software**   Many applications (e.g., [[0]], [[1]], [[2]]) support cross-platform shortcut customization. You can set the same shortcuts in these apps on different systems.   ## 2. **Modify System Shortcuts**   - **Windows**: Adjust shortcuts in `Settings > Devices > Keyboard`.   - **macOS**: Modify shortcuts in `System Preferences > Keyboard > Shortcuts`.   - **Linux**: Customize shortcuts in the desktop environment settings (e.g., GNOME, KDE).   ## 3. **Use Third-Party Tools**   Tools like [[3]] (Windows/macOS) or [[4]] (Linux) can remap keys to ensure consistency.   ## 4. **Leverage Scripts for Automation**   Write scripts (e.g., AutoHotkey for Windows, Karabiner-Elements for macOS) to unify shortcuts.   ## 5. **Consider Hardware Solutions**   Some keyboards (e.g., [[5]]) support onboard shortcut programming, allowing the same keybindings across systems.   ### **Notes**   - Some system-level shortcuts (e.g., `Cmd` vs. `Ctrl`) may require adaptation.   - Test changes to avoid conflicts with default shortcuts.   By following these methods, you can streamline your workflow across dual systems without relearning shortcuts.
subtitle: This article shares how I set up key mappings to seamlessly switch between Mac and Windows systems using the same muscle memory keys, along with insights on efficient input.

title: How to Develop iOS Apps with Cursor and Preview in Real Time   Developing iOS applications has become more efficient with tools like Cursor, which streamline coding and enable real-time previews. Below is a step-by-step guide to help you get started.   ### Prerequisites   - A Mac with macOS [[0]] or later   - Xcode [[1]] installed   - Basic knowledge of Swift [[2]] or Objective-C [[3]]   ### Step 1: Install Cursor   1. Download Cursor from [[4]].   2. Follow the installation instructions for macOS.   ### Step 2: Create a New Project   1. Open Cursor and select **New Project**.   2. Choose **iOS App** as the template.   3. Name your project and select Swift [[2]] or Objective-C [[3]] as the language.   ### Step 3: Configure the Project   1. Set the deployment target to iOS [[5]] or later.   2. Ensure **Use Core Data** or **Include Unit Tests** are selected if needed.   ### Step 4: Write Code with Real-Time Preview   1. Open the `ContentView.swift` [[6]] file (or equivalent for Objective-C).   2. Start coding your UI—Cursor supports SwiftUI [[7]] and UIKit [[8]].   3. Enable the **Live Preview** feature to see changes instantly.   ### Step 5: Run and Debug   1. Connect an iOS device or use the simulator.   2. Click **Run** to build and launch the app.   3. Debug issues using Cursor’s built-in tools or Xcode [[1]].   ### Tips for Efficiency   - Use keyboard shortcuts (e.g., `⌘ + R` to refresh the preview).   - Leverage Cursor’s AI-assisted coding for faster development.   - Regularly test on different iOS [[5]] versions for compatibility.   By following these steps, you can efficiently develop and preview iOS apps with Cursor. Happy coding!   For more details, visit [[9]].

title: How to Enable Dial-Up Internet After Disabling DHCP on TP-Link   When configuring a TP-Link router, you may need to disable its DHCP server to avoid conflicts in a network with multiple routers. However, this can prevent the router from automatically obtaining an IP address for internet access. Here's how to manually set up dial-up internet after disabling DHCP:   ### Steps to Configure   1. **Access the Router's Admin Page**      - Connect to the router via Wi-Fi or Ethernet.      - Open a browser and enter the router's management address (e.g., `192.168.1.1`).      - Log in with your admin credentials.   2. **Disable the DHCP Server**      - Navigate to **Network > DHCP Settings**.      - Set **DHCP Server** to **Disabled** and save the changes.   3. **Configure WAN Connection Manually**      - Go to **Network > WAN Settings**.      - Select **PPPoE** (or your ISP's required connection type).      - Enter your ISP-provided username and password.      - Set the connection mode to **Connect Automatically** or **Always On**.      - Save the settings.   4. **Restart the Router**      - Reboot the router to apply the changes.   5. **Verify Internet Access**      - After restarting, check if the router successfully connects to the internet.   ### Notes   - If your network uses static IPs, configure the router's LAN IP to avoid conflicts.   - Ensure the router's WAN port is connected to the modem or upstream network.   By following these steps, your TP-Link router should maintain internet access even with DHCP disabled.
subtitle: TP-Link's new routers have some peculiar limitations. For instance, if you disable the router's DHCP service, all ports will automatically switch to LAN ports.

title: TeslaMate User Guide
subtitle: An Introduction to Some Issues in the Installation and Usage of TeslaMate   STRICT RULES:   1. Output ONLY the translated English text. No explanations, no wrapping code fences.   2. A placeholder is ONLY a token of the exact form [[N]] (a number in DOUBLE square brackets, e.g. [[0]], [[1]]) that ALREADY appears verbatim in the input. Placeholders stand in for code, URLs, and tags that must NOT be translated.      Reproduce every input placeholder EXACTLY: same number, double square brackets [[ ]], no spaces inside, in a sensible position. Never drop, merge, renumber, or translate them. The SET of [[N]] tokens in your output MUST be identical to the input — same numbers, same count. Adjacent placeholders such as [[3]][[4]] must each appear as two separate tokens.   3. NEVER invent, fabricate, or add a [[N]] token, and NEVER turn any other text INTO placeholder form. Uppercase abbreviations or initials (e.g. KJ, FZ, HD, SQ), acronyms, single letters, names, and privacy redactions are ORDINARY TEXT — translate or transliterate them normally; do NOT replace them with [[N]]. If the input contains no [[N]] tokens, your output MUST contain none.   4. Preserve Markdown structure: headings (#), lists, blockquotes, tables, emphasis, line breaks and blank lines.   5. Do NOT alter anything a placeholder represents. Do NOT alter URLs, file paths, code, or HTML.   6. Keep technical terms accurate; translate prose naturally and fluently.

title: Arc: A "User-Friendly" Browser   The browser market has long been dominated by giants like Chrome, Firefox, and Safari, leaving little room for innovation. However, Arc [[0]] challenges this status quo with a fresh approach to browsing.   ### What Makes Arc Different?   1. **Vertical Tabs**: Arc moves tabs to the sidebar, freeing up vertical space and making navigation more intuitive.   2. **Spaces**: Organize your workflow with dedicated "Spaces" for work, personal use, or projects.   3. **Minimalist Design**: A clean interface with no clutter, focusing on what matters—your content.   4. **Built-in Tools**: Features like note-taking [[1]] and split-view [[2]] eliminate the need for extensions.   ### Why "User-Friendly"?   Arc prioritizes usability without sacrificing power. Its thoughtful design reduces distractions, while features like **Peek** (hover previews) and **Boosts** (custom styling for sites) enhance productivity.   ### The Catch   Currently invite-only [[3]], Arc is macOS-only [[4]], limiting its accessibility. Yet, for those who try it, the experience often feels like browsing reimagined.   If you're tired of conventional browsers, Arc might just be the breath of fresh air you need.   [[5]]
subtitle: Why Did I Decide to Make Arc My Default Browser After Just 1 Day of Use?

title: Why Do I Need a Hong Kong Phone Number   In today's interconnected world, having a Hong Kong phone number can offer numerous advantages, whether for business, travel, or personal use. Here are some key reasons why you might consider getting one:   ### 1. **Business and Professional Use**      - **Local Presence**: A Hong Kong number establishes credibility for businesses operating in or targeting the Hong Kong market.      - **Customer Trust**: Clients and partners are more likely to contact a local number than an international one.      - **Access to Local Services**: Some Hong Kong-based platforms or services may require a local number for verification.   ### 2. **Travel and Convenience**      - **Seamless Communication**: Avoid roaming charges by using a local SIM card while traveling in Hong Kong.      - **Ride-Hailing and Food Delivery**: Apps like [[0]] or [[1]] often require a local number for registration.   ### 3. **Privacy and Security**      - **Separate Identity**: Keep your primary number private by using a Hong Kong number for specific transactions.      - **Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)**: Secure your accounts with a dedicated number for verification codes.   ### 4. **Financial and Online Services**      - **Banking**: Some Hong Kong banks may require a local number for account setup or notifications.      - **E-Commerce**: Platforms like [[2]] or [[3]] might restrict certain features without a local number.   ### 5. **Long-Term Residency or Frequent Visits**      - **Utility Services**: Setting up utilities or subscriptions often requires a local contact number.      - **Social Integration**: A local number simplifies interactions with friends, colleagues, or service providers.   ### How to Get a Hong Kong Phone Number   You can obtain one through:   - **Local SIM Cards**: Purchase from providers like [[4]] or [[5]] at airports or stores.   - **Virtual Numbers**: Use VoIP services like [[6]] for a non-physical option.   Whether for short-term convenience or long-term needs, a Hong Kong phone number can be a valuable asset.   [[7]] [[8]]
subtitle: A Brief Introduction to Why I Need a Hong Kong Mobile Number   There are several reasons why I might want to obtain a Hong Kong mobile number:   1. **Business Needs** – If I have work or business dealings in Hong Kong, a local number makes communication with clients or partners more convenient and professional.   2. **Travel Convenience** – When visiting Hong Kong, a local number helps avoid high roaming charges and ensures stable connectivity.   3. **Online Services** – Some platforms or apps (e.g., [[0]], [[1]]) may require Hong Kong numbers for registration or verification.   4. **Privacy Protection** – Using a separate number for certain activities can help maintain privacy and reduce spam.   5. **Financial Services** – Opening bank accounts or applying for financial products in Hong Kong often requires a local contact number.   Whether for practicality or necessity, having a Hong Kong mobile number can be quite useful in various scenarios.

title: # The Craft of Markdown and GitHub Markdown   Markdown is a lightweight markup language widely used for formatting text. Its simplicity and readability make it popular for writing documentation, notes, and even blog posts. GitHub Markdown extends the basic syntax with additional features tailored for software development workflows.   ## Key Features of Markdown   - **Headers**: Use `#` for different levels of headings.   - **Emphasis**: `*italic*` or `_italic_`, `**bold**` or `__bold__`.   - **Lists**:     - Unordered lists with `-`, `*`, or `+`.     - Ordered lists with numbers.   - **Links and Images**:     - `[text](URL)` for links.     - `![alt text](image URL)` for images.   - **Code**:     - Inline code with backticks: `` `code` ``.     - Code blocks with triple backticks or indentation.   ## GitHub-Flavored Markdown (GFM)   GitHub enhances standard Markdown with features like:   - **Task Lists**: `- [x] Completed task` / `- [ ] Pending task`.   - **Tables**:     ```markdown     | Header 1 | Header 2 |     |----------|----------|     | Cell 1   | Cell 2   |     ```   - **Strikethrough**: `~~strikethrough text~~`.   - **Mentions & Issue References**: `@username`, `#issue-number`.   - **Syntax Highlighting**: Specify language after triple backticks (e.g., ```python).   ## Why Use Markdown?   - **Portability**: Plain text works everywhere.   - **Version Control Friendly**: Ideal for Git and GitHub.   - **Efficiency**: Focus on content, not formatting.   For more details, check [[0]] or explore [[1]]. Happy writing!

title: Migrating from Evernote to Apple's Built-in Notes App   For users looking to transition from Evernote to Apple's native Notes app, here's a simple guide to help you migrate your data smoothly.   ### Why Switch to Apple Notes?   1. **Seamless Integration**: Apple Notes syncs effortlessly across all Apple devices via iCloud.   2. **Privacy & Security**: Notes are end-to-end encrypted when stored in iCloud.   3. **Simplicity**: A clean, intuitive interface without unnecessary clutter.   ### How to Transfer Notes   1. **Export from Evernote**:      - Open Evernote and select the notes you want to migrate.      - Go to `File > Export` and choose the `ENEX` format.   2. **Import to Apple Notes**:      - Open the **Notes** app on your Mac.      - Navigate to `File > Import to Notes` and select the exported `.enex` file.   3. **Verify & Organize**:      - Check that all notes have been imported correctly.      - Use folders (or "Smart Folders" in macOS) to reorganize if needed.   ### Limitations to Note   - **Formatting**: Some complex formatting or embedded media may not transfer perfectly.   - **Tags**: Apple Notes doesn’t support Evernote-style tags, so consider using folders instead.   ### Final Tips   - For large libraries, migrate in batches to avoid issues.   - Test with a few notes first to ensure compatibility.   Enjoy the simplicity of Apple Notes! [[0]] [[1]]

title: A Personal Reflection on the Importance of Code Optimization and Work Experience   Recently, while working on a project, I encountered a performance issue that gave me a profound lesson on the significance of code optimization and the value of accumulated experience.   ### The Problem   During a routine feature development, I noticed that a particular function was taking an unusually long time to execute—sometimes even causing timeouts. At first glance, the logic seemed straightforward, so I couldn’t pinpoint the bottleneck.   ### Debugging Process   1. **Initial Analysis**: I added logging to measure execution time and found that a nested loop was the culprit. The outer loop iterated over a list of [[0]], while the inner loop processed [[1]] for each item.   2. **Optimization Attempt**: I tried reducing the loop complexity by pre-processing some data, but the improvement was marginal.   3. **Seeking Help**: A senior colleague reviewed my code and immediately spotted an inefficient database query inside the inner loop.   ### The Solution   By moving the query outside the loop and caching the results, we reduced the execution time from [[2]] seconds to [[3]] milliseconds. The fix was simple, but it required an understanding of how database operations scale with data volume—a lesson that only experience can teach.   ### Key Takeaways   - **Optimization Matters**: Even "working" code can be a liability if it’s inefficient.   - **Experience is Invaluable**: Seasoned developers often spot issues faster because they’ve seen similar patterns before.   - **Collaboration Helps**: Don’t hesitate to ask for code reviews; fresh perspectives can save hours of debugging.   This experience reinforced my belief that writing functional code is just the first step—optimizing it and learning from others are what make us better engineers.
tags: 技术, 工作经验
